frameworks.name: task-related response (TRR) framework
frameworks.one_line: fMRI-BOLD entrained to task timing absent local spiking explanation

frameworks.name: arousal modulation
frameworks.one_line: trial-to-trial arousal indexed by pupil and hemodynamics

frameworks.name: signal-detection / orientation discrimination
frameworks.one_line: psychophysical task used to probe early visual cortex responses

core_question: Do task-related hemodynamic responses in early visual cortex vary trial-to-trial with task difficulty and behavioral performance?
key_assumptions: TRRs are decoupled from local spiking and LFP
Ipsilateral cortex (away from stimulus rep) isolates non-stimulus-driven signals
Pupil size indexes arousal comparable to BOLD TRR
manipulations: Trial-by-trial behavioral accuracy and reaction time
key_findings: TRR amplitude scales with task difficulty
TRR amplitude tracks accuracy, RT, and lapses across trials
Modulations not explained by respiration, cardiac, or head motion
Pupil size shows similar difficulty/behavior modulation
interpretations: TRRs reflect arousal at single-trial timescale
TRRs covary with task and behavioral state variables beyond stimulus drive
